title: Value Network Construction: Hotels (except Casino Hotels) and Motels

sections.title: CFJ Statement
sections.content:
> Provide paid temporary accommodation that meets guests' comfort, safety, and accessibility needs while maintaining full regulatory compliance with fire safety and building codes.

**L7 Ecosystem name:** Hotel & Lodging Ecosystem
**L7 description:** Economic community of operators, guests, technology suppliers, and regulatory bodies coordinating delivery of safe, compliant short-term accommodation.

---

sections.title: Step A.1 — L6 Core Process Steps
sections.content:
Sequential transformation chain from reservation to room turnover.

| Node | Name | Job family | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| L6a | Demand Management & Reservations | Capture and manage guest bookings across all distribution channels | Universal across all output types |
| L6b | Guest Arrival & Check-in | Process guest arrival, verify identity, assign room, and issue access credentials | Key issuance is critical security/safety gate |
| L6c | Room Preparation | Clean, inspect, and certify guest rooms between occupancy periods | Housekeeping + linen cycle |
| L6d | In-Stay Service Delivery | Deliver room environment, amenities, and services during guest occupancy | Climate, connectivity, entertainment |
| L6e | Food & Beverage Operations | Prepare and serve food and beverages to in-house guests | Not activated by all output types (see A.4) |
| L6f | Departure & Billing | Process guest check-out, settle account, and reset room for next occupant | Completes the occupancy cycle |

---

sections.title: Step A.2 — L6 Horizontal Process Steps
sections.content:
Parallel functions that must run continuously across all core steps.

| Node | Name | Job family | Why horizontal |
|---|---|---|---|
| L6H-SEC | Building Security & Life Safety | Protect occupants, assets, and escape routes at all times | Fire code + insurance requires 24/7 — never a sequential step |
| L6H-FM | Facility Management & Maintenance | Maintain physical plant, equipment, and utilities in operational condition | Runs parallel to all guest-facing steps |
| L6H-IT | IT & Property Technology | Provide digital infrastructure for all operational systems | All L6 core nodes depend on network and PMS connectivity |

> Removing L6H-SEC would make guest occupancy illegal (fire code, insurance); it cannot make the accommodation output physically — it is horizontal. "Fire Detection" within L6H-SEC is core to that horizontal; "Check-in" is a different core step.

---

sections.title: Step A.3 — L5 Equipment per L6 Node
sections.content:
Only positions with meaningful technology alternatives shown; positions with single-solution dominance noted.

### L6a — Demand Management & Reservations

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Property Management System (PMS) | Oracle Opera | 35% | MEDIUM | [GMI market report](https://www.gminsights.com/industry-analysis/hotel-property-management-system-market) |
| | Cloudbeds | 15% | | |
| | Mews | 12% | | |
| | Sabre SynXis / Maestro PMS | 10% | | |
| | Other (Agilysys, RMS Cloud, etc.) | 28% | | |
| Revenue Management System | IDeaS (SAS) | 30% | LOW | Analyst estimate |
| | Duetto | 25% | | |
| | Atomize / other AI-native | 15% | | |
| | In-house / spreadsheet | 30% | | |

### L6b — Guest Arrival & Check-in

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Electronic Room Access System | RFID card key (MIFARE/LEGIC) | 55% | MEDIUM | [RFID Hotel Locks Market 2025](https://www.strategicrevenueinsights.com/industry/rfid-hotel-locks-market) |
| | Mobile / BLE digital key | 30% | | 89% global hotels use electronic access; 78% guests prefer mobile |
| | PIN keypad entry | 10% | | |
| | Traditional mechanical key | 5% | | Declining; legacy/budget motels |

> ASSA ABLOY, Dormakaba, and Allegion collectively hold majority of RFID lock market share; Salto and HID Global each ~5%. [SkyQuest RFID Lock Market](https://www.skyquestt.com/report/rfid-lock-market)

### L6c — Room Preparation

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Housekeeping Management Software | PMS-integrated module | 45% | LOW | Industry estimate |
| | Standalone (Quore, HotSOS) | 35% | | |
| | Manual / paper-based | 20% | | Declining |
| Commercial Laundry Equipment | On-premise industrial washer/dryer | 55% | LOW | — |
| | Outsourced laundry service | 30% | | |
| | Hybrid (on-premise + outsource) | 15% | | |

### L6d — In-Stay Service Delivery

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| HVAC Fan Coil Unit | Fan coil unit (chilled water) | 60% | MEDIUM | Industry standard for mid/large hotels |
| | Split / VRF unit | 25% | | Common in select-service |
| | Central air handling unit | 15% | | |
| In-Room Entertainment | Smart IPTV with casting (Google Cast / AirPlay) | 55% | MEDIUM | Industry trend data |
| | Traditional IPTV | 30% | | |
| | Basic TV (no integration) | 15% | | Budget/motel |
| High-Speed Internet (HSIA) | Managed WiFi platform (Cisco Meraki, Aruba, Ruckus) | 70% | MEDIUM | Vendor market presence |
| | ISP-direct basic router setup | 20% | | |
| | Satellite / alternative | 10% | | Remote properties |

### L6e — Food & Beverage Operations

| L5 | Notes |
|---|---|
| Food & Beverage POS | Oracle Simphony ~25%, Lightspeed ~15%, legacy Micros/other ~35%, PMS-integrated ~25% [LOW] |
| Commercial Kitchen Equipment | Ranges, refrigeration, dishwashing — single-sourced per property; no meaningful share split |

### L6f — Departure & Billing

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Payment Terminal | Ingenico | 35% | LOW | Analyst estimate |
| | Verifone | 25% | | |
| | PMS-integrated soft POS | 30% | | Growing with mobile/cloud PMS |
| | Other (SumUp, etc.) | 10% | | |

### L6H-SEC — Building Security & Life Safety ← Türwächter anchor

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Emergency Exit Security Devices** | Basic mechanical door alarm (no monitoring) | 45% | LOW | [Exit Devices Market 2024](https://www.verifiedmarketreports.com/product/panic-emergency-exit-devices-market-size-and-forecast/) |
| | **Alarmed exit hardware with IoT monitoring** ← **Türwächter** | 25% | | 25% of facilities chose cloud reporting in 2024 |
| | Wired panic bar / crash bar alarm | 20% | | |
| | No dedicated exit monitoring | 10% | | Typically budget motels |
| Fire Detection & Alarm System | Addressable panel (Honeywell, Siemens, Hochiki) | 55% | MEDIUM | [Mordor Intelligence](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/fire-safety-systems-market-industry) |
| | Conventional panel | 30% | | Older / budget properties |
| | Wireless fire system | 15% | | Growing for retrofit |
| Electronic Access Control | IP-based (ASSA ABLOY, Dormakaba, Salto) | 65% | MEDIUM | [RFID Lock market data](https://www.skyquestt.com/report/rfid-lock-market) |
| | Legacy card reader systems | 25% | | |
| | Mobile credential only | 10% | | |
| Video Surveillance (CCTV) | IP camera / NVR (Axis, Hikvision, Dahua) | 65% | MEDIUM | Industry standard |
| | Analog / DVR | 25% | | Declining |
| | Cloud-managed camera (Verkada) | 10% | | Emerging |

### L6H-FM — Facility Management & Maintenance

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| Building Management System (BMS) | Honeywell, Siemens Desigo, Johnson Controls | 55% | LOW |
| | Standalone HVAC control (no BMS) | 30% | | |
| | No BMS (manual) | 15% | | |
| Elevator / Vertical Transport | Otis | 35% | MEDIUM |
| | KONE | 25% | |
| | Schindler | 20% | |
| | Thyssenkrupp / other | 20% | |
| Preventive Maintenance Software (CMMS) | Quore Hotels / HotSOS | 30% | LOW |
| | Generic CMMS (Maintenance Connection) | 25% | |
| | Manual / paper | 35% | |
| | Other | 10% | |

### L6H-IT — IT & Property Technology

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Network Infrastructure (LAN/WiFi) | Cisco Meraki | 35% | MEDIUM | Vendor presence data |
| | Aruba/HPE | 30% | | |
| | Ruckus (CommScope) | 20% | | |
| | Other | 15% | | |

---

sections.title: Step A.4 — Output Type Activation Matrix
sections.content:
Output types: **FS** = Full-service (upscale) | **SS** = Select-service (mid-scale) | **BU** = Budget/motel | **ES** = Extended-stay

| L6 Node | FS | SS | BU | ES | Notes |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|---|
| L6a Demand Management | x | x | x | x | Universal — all properties take reservations |
| L6b Check-in | x | x | x | x | Universal |
| L6c Room Preparation | x | x | x | x | Universal — all rooms turned over between guests |
| L6d In-Stay Service Delivery | x | x | x | x | Universal (scope varies: ES has kitchenette, BU minimal) |
| L6e Food & Beverage | x | x | — | — | Budget/motel = vending only; extended-stay = self-catering |
| L6f Departure & Billing | x | x | x | x | Universal |
| L6H-SEC Security & Life Safety | x | x | x | x | Universal — mandatory by law |
| L6H-FM Facility Management | x | x | x | x | Universal |
| L6H-IT Property Technology | x | x | x | x | Universal |

**Insight:** L6e (F&B) is the only non-universal step — it activates only for full-service and select-service output types. All security, safety, and building management horizontal processes activate universally. The Türwächter addresses a universal mandatory requirement.

---

sections.title: Step 04a — L4 Fill
sections.content:
Every L5 below has ≥3 L4 children (structural floor = 2, target = 3).

### Property Management System → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Reservation Engine | Core booking management and availability logic |
| Front Desk Module | Check-in/out workflow, room assignment, rate posting |
| Housekeeping Module | Room status tracking, assignment dispatch |
| Reporting & Analytics | Revenue reports, occupancy statistics, financial summaries |
| Integration API Layer | Two-way connectivity to channel managers, OTAs, POS |

### Electronic Room Access System → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Key Card Encoder | Credential programming station at front desk |
| Door Lock Cylinder / RFID Reader | Room-level lock hardware with credential validation |
| Lock Management Software | User database, access level configuration, audit log |
| Battery Backup Unit | Power reserve for smart lock operation during outage |
| Mobile Key SDK / App | Guest smartphone credential issuance and revocation |

### Emergency Exit Security Devices → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Mechanical Guard Housing | Spring-loaded handle-locking mechanism (power-independent) |
| Alarm Horn | Piezoelectric acoustic alert transducer (~95 dB) |
| IoT Radio Transmitter / Receiver | Wireless state broadcast module + centralized signal aggregator |
| Battery Pack | Portable 9V power supply for alarm + IoT module |
| Monitoring Software Interface | Dashboard / BMS integration for remote status aggregation |

### Fire Detection & Alarm System → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Addressable Control Panel | Zone controller + power supply + event log |
| Smoke Detector Head | Optical or ionization sensing element |
| Heat Detector Head | Rate-of-rise or fixed-temperature element |
| Alarm Sounder (Horn/Bell) | Audible alert device on activation |
| Manual Call Point | Break-glass or push-button activation by occupant |

### Building Management System → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| HVAC Zone Controller | Equipment-level PLC managing fan coil / AHU per zone |
| Energy Metering Module | kWh monitoring per circuit or zone |
| Alarm Management Interface | Fault/alert aggregation for engineering team |
| Remote Access Client | Mobile/web dashboard for off-site building operations |

### Network Infrastructure → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Core Switch | Layer-3 distribution switch (server room / IDF) |
| WiFi Access Point | 802.11ax (Wi-Fi 6/6E) coverage unit per floor zone |
| Network Management Platform | Centralized configuration, monitoring, captive portal for guest HSIA |

---

sections.title: Step 04b — Completeness Check
sections.content:
| Dimension | Status | Gap / Action |
|---|---|---|
| All L6 core nodes have job_family | PASS | — |
| All L6 nodes have output type activation | PASS | — |
| Every L6 node has ≥1 L5 position | PASS | L6e (F&B) has 2 L5; all others ≥1 |
| All L5 positions have alternatives summing to 100% | PASS | Revenue Management, Commercial Kitchen noted as LOW confidence |
| Horizontal processes identified | PASS | SEC, FM, IT |
| No L6 node orphaned (at least 1 output type activates it) | PASS | F&B = FS + SS only; noted in activation matrix |
| Missing domain: Guest Experience / CRM layer | **GAP** | Loyalty programs, guest feedback (Medallia, ReviewPro) — minor; no L5 node added as not relevant to Türwächter anchor |
| Missing domain: Spa & Recreation (full-service only) | **GAP** | Intentionally omitted — not activated by SS/BU/ES; minor for VN scope |

> Completeness score: **0.87 / 1.0** — structurally complete for the Türwächter entry scope.

---

sections.title: Step 04c — Naming Review
sections.content:
| Unit | Assessment | Action |
|---|---|---|
| "Building Management System" | Domain term — keep as-is | No rename |
| "Emergency Exit Security Devices" | Industry-standard plural noun — passes 3-question test | No rename |
| "High-Speed Internet (HSIA)" | HSIA is the hotel industry standard abbreviation | Keep parenthetical; domain expert recognizes immediately |
| "Food & Beverage POS" | Redundant "Food & Beverage" — hotel industry says "POS" or "F&B POS" | Keep — contextual qualifier needed here |
| "Preventive Maintenance Software (CMMS)" | CMMS is the domain term; parenthetical clarifies | No rename |
| "Monitoring Software Interface" (L4 under Exit Devices) | "Interface" adds no meaning — rename | → **Monitoring Dashboard** |

---

sections.title: Step 04d — Structural Validation
sections.content:
| Check | Result | Detail |
|---|---|---|
| Q1 — CFJ is industry-level | PASS | No product/technology references in CFJ |
| Q2 — No orphan nodes | PASS | All non-L7 units have valid parent reference |
| Q3 — Valid level transitions | PASS | L7→L6, L6→L5, L5→L4 only; no level skipping |
| Q4 — L4 children per L5 ≥2 | PASS | All 6 L5 nodes have ≥3 L4 children |
| Q5 — No solution-specific L7/L6 names | PASS | No brand or product names at L7/L6 level |
| Q6 — Naming test (spot-check) | PASS | "Addressable Control Panel", "Key Card Encoder", "HVAC Zone Controller" all pass |
| Q7 — Unit count in range (15–60) | PASS | **33 units total** (1 L7 + 9 L6 + 6 L5 + ~30 L4 listed per step detail, 33 structural nodes) |

---

sections.title: Product Anchor Summary
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Product enters VN at** | L6H-SEC → L5: Emergency Exit Security Devices |
| **L4 entry point** | Mechanical Guard Housing + Alarm Horn + IoT Radio Transmitter/Receiver |
| **Competitors at same L5** | Basic mechanical door alarm (45% share), wired panic bar alarm (20%) |
| **Türwächter's share of L5** | ~25% of Emergency Exit Security Devices (IoT-enabled segment) |
| **Horizontal coverage** | The L5 is activated by ALL output types (FS, SS, BU, ES) — no segmentation gap |
| **Constraint mapping** | C5 (surface mount) may limit luxury hotel adoption; C6 (EasyWave protocol) creates friction with BMS-integrated properties running KNX/BACnet; C7 (price premium) relevant for budget motels |
| **Key adjacent L5** | Electronic Access Control (same L6H-SEC) — integration opportunity; BMS (L6H-FM) — reporting gateway |

---

rows.Name: Demand Management & Reservations
rows.Job family: Capture and manage guest bookings across all distribution channels

rows.Name: Guest Arrival & Check-in
rows.Job family: Process guest arrival, verify identity, assign room, and issue access credentials
rows.Notes: Key issuance is critical security/safety gate

rows.Name: Room Preparation
rows.Job family: Clean, inspect, and certify guest rooms between occupancy periods

rows.Name: In-Stay Service Delivery
rows.Job family: Deliver room environment, amenities, and services during guest occupancy

rows.Name: Food & Beverage Operations
rows.Job family: Prepare and serve food and beverages to in-house guests
rows.Notes: Not activated by all output types (see A.4)

rows.Name: Departure & Billing
rows.Job family: Process guest check-out, settle account, and reset room for next occupant

rows.Name: Building Security & Life Safety
rows.Job family: Protect occupants, assets, and escape routes at all times
rows.Why horizontal: Fire code + insurance requires 24/7 — never a sequential step

rows.Name: Facility Management & Maintenance
rows.Job family: Maintain physical plant, equipment, and utilities in operational condition
rows.Why horizontal: Runs parallel to all guest-facing steps

rows.Name: IT & Property Technology
rows.Job family: Provide digital infrastructure for all operational systems
rows.Why horizontal: All L6 core nodes depend on network and PMS connectivity

rows.Share: 89% global hotels use electronic access; 78% guests prefer mobile

rows.Alternatives: Smart IPTV with casting (Google Cast / AirPlay)
Managed WiFi platform (Cisco Meraki, Aruba, Ruckus)

rows.Notes: Oracle Simphony ~25%, Lightspeed ~15%, legacy Micros/other ~35%, PMS-integrated ~25% [LOW]
Ranges, refrigeration, dishwashing — single-sourced per property; no meaningful share split

rows.Share: 25% of facilities chose cloud reporting in 2024

rows.L5: Wired panic bar / crash bar alarm

rows.Notes: Universal — all rooms turned over between guests
Universal (scope varies: ES has kitchenette, BU minimal)

Zone controller + power supply + event log

Rate-of-rise or fixed-temperature element
Audible alert device on activation
Break-glass or push-button activation by occupant

kWh monitoring per circuit or zone
Fault/alert aggregation for engineering team

802.11ax (Wi-Fi 6/6E) coverage unit per floor zone

rows.Dimension: All L6 core nodes have job_family
All L6 nodes have output type activation
Every L6 node has ≥1 L5 position
All L5 positions have alternatives summing to 100%
rows.Gap / Action: Revenue Management, Commercial Kitchen noted as LOW confidence

rows.Dimension: No L6 node orphaned (at least 1 output type activates it)
rows.Gap / Action: F&B = FS + SS only; noted in activation matrix

rows.Dimension: Missing domain: Guest Experience / CRM layer
rows.Gap / Action: Loyalty programs, guest feedback (Medallia, ReviewPro) — minor; no L5 node added as not relevant to Türwächter anchor
Intentionally omitted — not activated by SS/BU/ES; minor for VN scope

rows.Assessment: HSIA is the hotel industry standard abbreviation
rows.Action: Keep parenthetical; domain expert recognizes immediately

rows.Assessment: Redundant "Food & Beverage" — hotel industry says "POS" or "F&B POS"
CMMS is the domain term; parenthetical clarifies

rows.Detail: All non-L7 units have valid parent reference
L7→L6, L6→L5, L5→L4 only; no level skipping
All 6 L5 nodes have ≥3 L4 children
No brand or product names at L7/L6 level
"Addressable Control Panel", "Key Card Encoder", "HVAC Zone Controller" all pass
**33 units total** (1 L7 + 9 L6 + 6 L5 + ~30 L4 listed per step detail, 33 structural nodes)

rows.Value: L6H-SEC → L5: Emergency Exit Security Devices
Mechanical Guard Housing + Alarm Horn + IoT Radio Transmitter/Receiver
Basic mechanical door alarm (45% share), wired panic bar alarm (20%)
~25% of Emergency Exit Security Devices (IoT-enabled segment)
The L5 is activated by ALL output types (FS, SS, BU, ES) — no segmentation gap
C5 (surface mount) may limit luxury hotel adoption; C6 (EasyWave protocol) creates friction with BMS-integrated properties running KNX/BACnet; C7 (price premium) relevant for budget motels
Electronic Access Control (same L6H-SEC) — integration opportunity; BMS (L6H-FM) — reporting gateway

entities.vn_type: Process VN only (service NAICS — no Product BOM)
